summary: Three required tests are implemented correctly and cover all acceptance criteria. Platform guard mocking is correct, subprocess args match the implementation exactly, and all tests run on Linux without real netsh calls. Minor cleanup items: two unused imports and one fragile string-match assertion.

suggestions.suggestion: Remove unused top-level `import subprocess`. All subprocess interactions are mocked via `patch`; the raw module is never referenced directly in tests.
suggestions.benefit: Cleaner imports, no false signal that subprocess is used directly.

suggestions.suggestion: Remove `call` from the `unittest.mock` import — it is imported but never used in any test in this file.
suggestions.benefit: Removes dead import; avoids confusion about intended usage.

suggestions.suggestion: Replace `f"name={expected_name}" in str(c)` with a direct args check: `any(call_args.args[0][-1] == f"name={expected_name}" for call_args in delete_calls)`. The `str(c)` approach is fragile — the `call` repr format is not part of the public API and could theoretically change.
suggestions.benefit: More robust assertion that relies on structured data rather than string repr.
